commands.add-novel: Usage: root add-novel [OPTIONS] TITLE START_URL Creates a novel in the library plus its first source, and prints the new ids. Arguments * title TEXT Novel title [required] * start_url TEXT Starting chapter URL of the first source [required] Options --adapter -a TEXT Scraper adapter key (see `adapters` in scraper/adapters.py) [default: fenrirealm] --lang -l TEXT Source language code [default: en] --raw Source is raw (foreign-language; needs translation) --offset FLOAT Add this to source-local numbers to get global chapter numbers [default: 0.0] --codex Enable the spoiler-safe codex for this novel --help Show this message and exit.
commands.chunk: Usage: root chunk [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Splits readable chapter text into overlapping, within-chapter passage chunks. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options --force -f Force re-chunking even if chunks exist --from FLOAT Only chunk chapters >= this number --to FLOAT Only chunk chapters <= this number --help Show this message and exit.
commands.embed: Usage: root embed [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Generates vector embeddings for any chunks missing them. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options --from FLOAT Only embed chunks in chapters >= this number --to FLOAT Only embed chunks in chapters <= this number --help Show this message and exit.
commands.extract: Usage: root extract [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Performs forward-only structured entity/fact extraction in chronological order. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options --force -f Force re-extraction of chapters --from FLOAT Only extract chapters >= this number --to FLOAT Only extract chapters <= this number (e.g. iterate on the first 50) --help Show this message and exit.
commands.import: Usage: root import [OPTIONS] PATH Imports an EPUB or digital PDF into the library from the terminal (parse → segment → commit), mirroring the web import worker. Heuristic segmentation only (no interactive review). Scanned PDFs need the OCR confirm gate — import those from the web UI. Arguments * path TEXT Path to an .epub or digital .pdf file to import [required] Options --novel -n INTEGER Append to this existing novel id (omit to create a new novel) --offset FLOAT Append: add this to segment chapter numbers to get global numbers [default: 0.0] --codex Build the codex over the imported range after committing --help Show this message and exit.
commands.import-batch: Usage: root import-batch [OPTIONS] FOLDER Bulk-imports every EPUB/digital-PDF under a folder. With --series, books sharing a detected series become one multi-volume novel; otherwise each book becomes its own novel. Arguments * folder TEXT Folder to scan recursively for .epub/.pdf (e.g. a Calibre library) [required] Options --series Group EPUB volumes that share a series into single novels --codex Build the codex over each imported novel afterward --help Show this message and exit.
commands.import-series: Usage: root import-series [OPTIONS] PATHS... Imports several volumes as a single multi-volume novel (one source per volume). Arguments * paths PATHS... EPUB/PDF volumes to fold into one novel (ordered by detected series index) [required] Options --codex Build the codex over the new novel afterward --help Show this message and exit.
commands.import-worker: Usage: root import-worker [OPTIONS] Runs the durable import worker as a standalone process (parse/OCR/commit jobs from the DB queue). Use this to split the worker off the web image; Ctrl-C stops it cleanly. Options --help Show this message and exit.
commands.merge: Usage: root merge [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Deduplicates and merges two duplicate entities in the database. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options * --keep -k INTEGER The entities.id of the entity to KEEP [required] * --drop -d INTEGER The entities.id of the duplicate entity to MERGE and DELETE [required] --help Show this message and exit.
commands.rebuild-bm25: Usage: root rebuild-bm25 [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Rebuilds and persists the per-novel sparse BM25 lexical search index. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options --help Show this message and exit.
commands.reset-codex: Usage: root reset-codex [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Deletes derived structured Codex data but preserves chapter chunks/embeddings. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options --force -f Reset without confirmation --help Show this message and exit.
commands.reset-db: Usage: root reset-db [OPTIONS] Resets the entire database by dropping all tables, data, and re-applying schema. Options --force -f Force reset without interactive prompt --help Show this message and exit.
commands.scrape: Usage: root scrape [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Scrapes a novel's sources chapter by chapter (stops cleanly at premium). Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id to scrape (all its sources) [required] Options --source -s INTEGER Scrape only this source id --force -f Force scrape already existing chapters --max -m INTEGER Maximum number of chapters to scrape in this run --help Show this message and exit.
commands.translate: Usage: root translate [OPTIONS] NOVEL_ID Translates raw (foreign-language) chapters into English, growing the name glossary. Arguments * novel_id INTEGER The novel id [required] Options --from FLOAT Only translate chapters >= this number --to FLOAT Only translate chapters <= this number --force -f Re-translate even if already translated --seed Seed the glossary from codex entities first --help Show this message and exit.

main: Usage: root [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]... CLI management engine for the novel reading platform. Options --install-completion Install completion for the current shell. --show-completion Show completion for the current shell, to copy it or customize the installation. --help Show this message and exit. Commands add-novel Creates a novel in the library plus its first source, and prints the new ids. scrape Scrapes a novel's sources chapter by chapter (stops cleanly at premium). chunk Splits readable chapter text into overlapping, within-chapter passage chunks. embed Generates vector embeddings for any chunks missing them. extract Performs forward-only structured entity/fact extraction in chronological order. translate Translates raw (foreign-language) chapters into English, growing the name glossary. import Imports an EPUB or digital PDF into the library from the terminal (parse → segment → commit), mirroring the web import worker. Heuristic segmentation only (no interactive review). Scanned PDFs need the OCR confirm gate — import those from the web UI. import-batch Bulk-imports every EPUB/digital-PDF under a folder. With --series, books sharing a detected series become one multi-volume novel; otherwise each book becomes its own novel. import-series Imports several volumes as a single multi-volume novel (one source per volume). import-worker Runs the durable import worker as a standalone process (parse/OCR/commit jobs from the DB queue). Use this to split the worker off the web image; Ctrl-C stops it cleanly. rebuild-bm25 Rebuilds and persists the per-novel sparse BM25 lexical search index. merge Deduplicates and merges two duplicate entities in the database. reset-codex Deletes derived structured Codex data but preserves chapter chunks/embeddings. reset-db Resets the entire database by dropping all tables, data, and re-applying schema.
